Its core capabilities include:\\n- **Parse**: Layout-aware document parsing (PDFs, spreadsheets, scans).\\n- **Extract**: Schema-guided data extraction.\\n- **Split**: Intelligent document chunking/splitting.\\n- **Classify**: Automated document classification.\\n- **Edit**: Programmatic form filling and document modification.\\n\\n### 2. Public API & Documentation\\n- **Canonical Docs URL**: https://docs.reducto.ai/\\n- **API Reference**: https://docs.reducto.ai/api-reference/\\n- **SDKs**: Supports Python and Node.js SDKs.\\n\\n### 3. raw_result.description: AI document-processing platform for layout-aware parsing, extraction, classification, splitting, and editing of files via API, SDKs, and MCP.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs show API authentication with a Bearer token in the Authorization header and SDK setup via the REDUCTO_API_KEY environment variable; docs also describe CLI/MCP --login session-based authentication.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing page offers a self-serve Standard tier with the first 15,000 credits free, then usage-based pricing; Growth and Enterprise are custom.

api_surface.breadth: Public API docs cover multiple document workflows including parse, extract, classify, split, edit, job polling, rate limits, throttling, and official Python/Node/Go SDKs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP documentation exists on Reducto docs, and Reducto maintains a public mcp-server-reducto repository under its official GitHub organization.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs state sustained edge rate limits of 1,000 requests/second across all endpoints and 200 requests/second for GET job status polling; docs also describe a concurrency throttle with baseline capacities by tier.
raw_result.notable_signals: Well-developed first-party developer surface: public docs, API reference, rate-limit and throttling docs, usage-based self-serve pricing, official SDKs, and an official MCP server.
